description:
You are acting as an automation-workflow specialist.  
A small project already exists on disk:

  /home/user/project/data/raw/            (directory already present)
      ├── report1.txt                     (contains the uppercase word “CONFIDENTIAL”)
      ├── report2.txt                     (does NOT contain that word)
      ├── secret_notes.txt                (contains the uppercase word “CONFIDENTIAL”)
      ├── analysis.txt                    (contains the uppercase word “CONFIDENTIAL”)
      └── draft.log                       (arbitrary log – ignore it)

Goal  
1. Search, inside /home/user/project/data/raw, for every “*.txt” file that contains the exact, case-sensitive string CONFIDENTIAL.  
2. Copy each matching file into /home/user/project/data/processed (create that directory if it is missing) while renaming the copy by appending “_secured” to the original base-name, just before the “.txt” extension.  
   • Example: report1.txt → report1_secured.txt  
3. After copying, create a log file named /home/user/project/data/processed/processed_log.txt that precisely records which files were handled.  
   • One line per processed file.  
   • The line format that the automated test will look for is:  
         ORIGINAL_FULL_PATH -> NEW_FULL_PATH  
   • Use absolute (full) paths exactly as they appear on disk.  
   • Sort the lines alphabetically by ORIGINAL_FULL_PATH (simple byte sort, same result as the default sort(1) command).  

Technical requirement  
• Complete the whole job using “find … | xargs …” style batch commands; no loops written in the shell.  
• Only a small sequence (≈3–4) of terminal commands should achieve the goal.  
• No sudo/root rights are available.  
• Keep original files unchanged; only copies with the new names must appear in the processed directory.  
• Do not quote or print the exact commands in your reply; simply run them inside the terminal.  

When finished, verify that:  
• /home/user/project/data/processed now exists and contains exactly the newly named copies plus the processed_log.txt file.  
• processed_log.txt follows the required format and contains one correctly sorted line per file handled.  

If everything matches, the task is complete.
truth:
Initial filesystem (before the agent starts):

/home/user/project/data/raw/report1.txt
    Q1 RESULTS
    CONFIDENTIAL
    Numbers …  

/home/user/project/data/raw/report2.txt
    Public release – nothing confidential.  

/home/user/project/data/raw/secret_notes.txt
    CONFIDENTIAL
    Internal roadmap …  

/home/user/project/data/raw/analysis.txt
    Executive summary
    CONFIDENTIAL data enclosed …  

/home/user/project/data/raw/draft.log
    [log lines]

Desired end-state after correct agent work:

Directory:
/home/user/project/data/processed/              (permissions 755 or inherited)

Files inside “processed”:

1. /home/user/project/data/processed/report1_secured.txt
   ── exact byte-for-byte copy of raw/report1.txt

2. /home/user/project/data/processed/secret_notes_secured.txt
   ── exact byte-for-byte copy of raw/secret_notes.txt

3. /home/user/project/data/processed/analysis_secured.txt
   ── exact byte-for-byte copy of raw/analysis.txt

4. /home/user/project/data/processed/processed_log.txt
   Contents (3 lines terminated by Unix LF, final newline optional):

/home/user/project/data/raw/analysis.txt -> /home/user/project/data/processed/analysis_secured.txt
/home/user/project/data/raw/report1.txt -> /home/user/project/data/processed/report1_secured.txt
/home/user/project/data/raw/secret_notes.txt -> /home/user/project/data/processed/secret_notes_secured.txt

(Alphabetical order by ORIGINAL path; no extra spaces except the single literal space before and after “->”.)

Files that must NOT be present in processed/: report2_secured.txt, draft.log, anything else.

The automated test will check:
• Existence of the processed directory.
• Presence and byte-equality of the three “*_secured.txt” copies against their originals.
• That processed_log.txt exists exactly as specified above (name, location, line format, ordering).
• That no additional unexpected files reside in /home/user/project/data/processed.
